review_questions: Does CP00-147 cover exactly the 150 planned Risk C permission matrix continuation and failure taxonomy reference units in order?
Can any CP00-147 permission matrix row evaluate decisions, apply legal hold or ethical wall, read object ACL, route review or approval, prove trimming, emit audit events, or execute permission tests?

Can any CP00-147 failure taxonomy row evaluate a taxonomy, throw failures, execute recovery, retry exhaustion, rollback, compensation, fixtures, unit tests, integration smokes, blocked-claim receipts, or Hermes failure evidence?
Are hidden source fields, unauthorized counts, object names, internal permission policies, failure recovery state, rollback state, compensation plans, and blocked-claim internals suppressed?
Does CP00-147 hand off cleanly from CP00-146 to CP00-148 without implementing LDIP or splitting HRX?

findings.title: Core catalog implementation source is untracked and absent from the reviewed diff
findings.details: The diff under review adds the re-export in packages/audit/src/index.js, the contract public-export list, the tests, and the validator, all of which depend on permission-matrix-failure-taxonomy-reference-catalog.js. That file is shown as untracked in git status and its contents are not rendered in the diff (git diff omits untracked files), so the descriptor/reference-only and no-write/no-execute behavior of all 150 units is verified only indirectly. Indirect evidence is strong and consistent: node --check on the file passed; node --test --test-name-pattern='CP00-147' passed (62 tests, 0 failures) asserting the full no-write/no-evaluate/no-execute surface across 11 representative cases spanning RP03.P06.M06.S04 through RP03.P07.M03.S14 (permission matrix, mutation/share/AI-retrieval decisions, legal hold, ethical wall, object ACL, review/approval routing, security trimming, fixtures, failure taxonomy, recovery, retry/rollback/compensation, blocked-claim and Hermes failure evidence, audit failure hint, UI/API/network/Claude, LDIP, HRX, and count/object-name exposure); contract no_write_attestation and hidden_source_fields are comprehensive; deliverable_counts (40+26+17+4+16+44+2+1) and evidence_mode_counts (19+22+22+20+11+11+11+20+14) each sum to 150; range, order, and uniqueness are validated; and npm run rp03:audit:validate reported current_pack CP00-147, covered_units 150, next_pack CP00-148. The local-validation note 'unrelated pre-existing untracked files only' does not appear to account for this pack-owned file.
findings.recommendation: Stage and commit packages/audit/src/permission-matrix-failure-taxonomy-reference-catalog.js as part of CP00-147 (currently untracked) so the index.js re-export does not resolve to a missing module, and include the source in the review scope so the no-write/no-execute construction can be audited directly rather than solely through the passing test suite and validator.

required_adjudication: Confirm the currently-untracked core source file packages/audit/src/permission-matrix-failure-taxonomy-reference-catalog.js is committed with CP00-147; the index.js re-export, the contract permission_matrix_failure_taxonomy_reference_public_exports list, the test imports, and the validator all depend on it, and the build breaks for others if it is omitted.

Acknowledge that the descriptor/reference-only and no-write/no-execute guarantees for all 150 CP00-147 units (RP03.P06.M06.S04-RP03.P07.M03.S14) are verified indirectly (62 passing CP00-147 tests, node --check pass, validator covered_units 150, next_pack CP00-148) because the catalog implementation is not rendered in the reviewed diff.
